SPY $709.00 +0.48 (+0.1%)QQQ $646.24 -1.67 (-0.3%)VIX 19.27 +0.32 (+1.7%)GEX $2592.4B +2365.8B
SPY hits extreme negative gamma territory while skew compresses across major indices, signaling potential volatility regime shift.
WHAT CHANGED
SPY gamma flipped to extreme negative levels at -$1.07B (79th percentile), creating heightened sensitivity to directional moves despite modest +0.07% gain
Massive straddle activity in SPY with 187K calls and 279K puts at the 710/709/708 strikes, suggesting hedging ahead of volatility expansion
Skew compression hitting multi-asset (SPY, QQQ, NVDA all showing 2nd percentile readings) indicates options market pricing reduced tail risk despite negative gamma setup
Tech weakness emerging with QQQ lagging SPY by 33bps while VIX creeps higher to 19.27
NOTABLE DIVERGERS
IBIT and KWEB both at 96th percentile IV rank, significantly elevated vs crypto/China equity calm
EEM, XLY, and IWM all showing 83rd+ percentile IV despite relatively contained spot moves
The negative gamma combined with compressed skew creates an unusual setup where small moves could amplify quickly, yet the market isn't pricing significant tail risk.

Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 10:36 ET
Dispersion play materializes as index puts meet single-name call euphoria
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. The 0.67 P/C divergence (index 1.44 vs single-name 0.77) signals institutional dispersion hedging into greed regime.
  • 2. Low implied correlation at 0.04 confirms stock-specific regime — single-name vol trading more efficiently than index.
  • 3. TSLA earnings in 2 days plus GDP Thursday create volatility catalyst window amid positive dealer gamma.
NARRATIVE — greed with hedge demand

The most striking signal today is the extreme P/C divergence between index hedging and single-name speculation, with institutions buying index puts (1.44 ratio) while retail chases individual stock calls (0.77 ratio) — a textbook dispersion setup. This 0.67 spread represents the widest hedge/speculation gap in recent weeks, occurring precisely as implied correlation collapsed to 0.04, confirming the market has entered a stock-specific regime where individual names can diverge dramatically from index performance. Dealer positioning amplifies this dynamic with SPY holding $+2.34B positive gamma — meaning systematic hedging will dampen broad market moves while allowing single-name volatility to flourish. The unusual call flow in USAR (+1025%), BULL (+618%), and HIMS (+536%) reflects speculative positioning ahead of TSLA earnings Tuesday and GDP Thursday, with traders betting on individual catalysts rather than macro direction. Six names showing rich vol/call heavy divergence (WMT, CSCO, PANW among others) suggest option buyers are overpaying for bullish exposure just as technical momentum peaks — 9 major names hit RSI >70 including SPY itself. The combination of stretched technicals, positive dealer gamma, and imminent macro catalysts creates a window where single-name volatility could spike while index volatility remains suppressed.

Layer 1: Sentiment +55Layer 2: GEX +$2.3BLayer 3: P/C divergence 0.67Layer 4: IC 0.04
